channel.title: The Grant Desk - Worldwide, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Worldwide, next 30 days.

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Web Residencies No. 22 - Ignore All Previous Instructions
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Online (fully remote). Awarded by Akademie Schloss Solitude, Stuttgart, Germany.

Award: EUR 1,200 grant for a four-week online residency, plus a microsite to develop and publish the project, and support from a web/UX designer and a programmer in a supportive capacity.

Duration: Four-week online residency.

Deadline: 07 Aug 2026

Web Residencies No. 22, 'Ignore All Previous Instructions,' is curated by !Mediengruppe Bitnik as part of Akademie Schloss Solitude's Web Residencies program, which since 2016 has supported young talents from the international digital scene as well as artists of all disciplines engaging with web-based practices. For the tenth anniversary of the program, this edition invites artists to create works that challenge extractive digital platforms and AI systems, exploring how to interrupt dominant technological logics, from ad-tech surveillance to prompt injection vulnerabilities, and imagine alternative structures and communication methods. The open call seeks proposals that hijack, glitch, and disrupt rather than comply with existing systems, drawing on strategies like Situationist detournement to map routes through and around the grids we are trapped within. Four to six proposals will be selected. BENEFITS: a four-week online residency; a grant of EUR 1,200; a web space (microsite) on which the project can be developed and published online; support from a web/UX designer and a programmer (in a supportive capacity only, not to build an entire website); and participation in individual and group online events with the curators and Solitude fellows. Offline or hybrid formats (a reading session, panel, exhibition, or curated program) may follow. ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines may apply, with no age limit; submission is free of charge. The residency takes place exclusively online, and selected fellows agree to be available online during the four weeks. TIMELINE: the open call runs from July 7 to August 7, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mountainfilm: Commitment Grants & Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Mountainfilm

Location: Application online; Telluride, Colorado, United States (fellowship trip during the festival). Commitment Grants are open to filmmakers from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship requires current U.S. residency.

Award: Over USD 90,000 in total funding. Individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. The Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship adds a yearlong mentorship, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a festival pass).

Duration: Commitment Grants support projects in post-production; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship is a yearlong mentorship.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Mountainfilm's Commitment Grants support documentary filmmakers with projects in post-production, with over USD 90,000 in funding this cycle for films that celebrate adventure, activism, social justice, culture, the environment, and indomitable spirit. Mountainfilm actively encourages diversity behind the camera, and a portion of funding is set aside for two focus areas: projects featuring an LGBTQ+ protagonist or a story about overcoming personal challenges, societal stigmas, and/or community obstacles with a meaningful connection to the outdoors or nature-based pursuits; and projects exploring immigration or migration, or created by immigrant or first-generation immigrant filmmakers. AWARDS: individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. EMERGING FILMMAKER FELLOWSHIP: one early-career filmmaker is selected from the Commitment Grant applicant pool for a yearlong mentorship that includes edit and story consultation, networking opportunities, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride during the festival over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a Mountainfilm pass, applicable to the year following the award). ELIGIBILITY: Commitment Grants are open to applicants from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship accepts applicants from any country but requires current U.S. residency. Post-production projects only, completed, released, or festival-screened projects are not eligible, and impact campaigns are not funded. Prior Commitment Grant recipients should not re-apply for the same project; previously declined projects may re-apply only with substantial changes. REQUIREMENTS: a video sample or trailer of the current project no longer than 10 minutes (password-protected Vimeo links preferred); the screener link must stay active and cannot be changed after submission. The Emerging Filmmaker application additionally requires a director bio, headshot, and cover letter. Recipients are expected to provide progress updates 2-3 times a year, include Mountainfilm recognition in credits, and discuss premiere consideration with the programming team. TIMELINE: applications are open July 1 to July 16, 2026 (closing 11:59 PM MDT), with notifications in late August or early September. Questions: entries@mountainfilm.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ecoslay Mag: Open Call for Contributions, Issue 02 "The Urban Issue"
item.description:
Organisation: Ecoslay Mag

Location: Remote / international. Two online workshops with the editorial team; contributions delivered remotely.

Award: Financial compensation for selected contributions (amount unspecified). Editorial support, proofreading, and graphic design provided. No application fee.

Duration: Contributions developed approx. 1 September 2026 to 10 January 2027, with two online workshops (first around 15 September 2026). Final contribution deadline approx. 10 January 2027.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Ecoslay Mag invites contributions for the second print issue of Ecoslay, "The Urban Issue," exploring the city as a space where intersectional ecological futures are negotiated, contested, resisted, and reinvented. In the Ecoslay way, pop culture and its influence are analysed and hijacked to communicate socio-ecological issues and ignite systemic change: what happens when climate politics meets internet culture, urban ecology meets reality TV, and sustainability discourse collides with camp, irony, humour, and memes. FORMATS (including but not limited to): essays, interviews, science communication, poems, visual art, documentation of (art)works, and fiction. TOPICS: urban climate communication through humour, memes, and pop culture; greenwashing, eco-branding, and sustainable aesthetics in urban contexts; urban farming, more-than-human life, housing crises, displacement and green gentrification, class and the city, maintenance and care work in relation to urbanism, urban environmental justice, and community organizing, and how these relate to pop culture; and reflections on what Ecoslay means in an age of overlapping crises. COMMITMENT: selected participants deliver one contribution to Issue 02 and attend two online workshops with the editorial team and fellow contributors for communal exchange and feedback on proposals and works-in-progress. WHAT'S OFFERED: financial compensation for selected contributions, editorial support, and edition, proofreading, and graphic design handled by the team. TIMELINE: open call closes 13 July 2026; selected contributors announced approx. 15 August 2026; work on contributions approx. 1 September 2026 to 10 January 2027; contributor workshop approx. 15 September 2026; final contribution deadline approx. 10 January 2027. HOW TO APPLY: submit via the Google form. Questions via ecoslay@protonmail.com or Instagram DM @ecoslay_mag.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Emergence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: New Emergence Art

Location: International; open worldwide. Shortlist shown in an online exhibition.

Award: Prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize cash award, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee.

Duration: One-off prize; shortlisted artists shown in an online exhibition 11 August to 7 September 2026

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The New Emergence Art Prize 2026 is an international open call for emerging artists, presented by New Emergence Art. There is no set theme and work may be submitted in any medium. AWARDS: a prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards supporting artists through cash prizes, artist materials, mentoring, and a residency, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to emerging artists; entrants may submit up to three original artworks that represent the quality, originality and direction of their practice. Final selections are made by an independent judging panel considering concept, execution, originality and impact. Shortlisted artists are featured in an online exhibition running 11 August to 7 September 2026. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee (amount set at the entry step). DEADLINE: 3 August 2026, 23:59 BST. Apply via https://www.newemergenceart.com/apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Club Nothing: Community Music Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Award: USD 40,000 fund total; four individuals or collectives each receive USD 10,000 to produce a club event, series, or festival. No application fee.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. Learn more and apply at nothing.tech/club-nothing (details also posted at https://nothing.community/d/59214-want-10000-to-throw-a-party). See full terms at nothing.tech/club-nothing/terms-and-conditions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Textile Exchange x Atmos: Fashion Regenerated 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Textile Exchange (with Atmos)

Location: Open call; work to be promoted globally through Textile Exchange and Atmos and exhibited at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference.

Award: Overall winner: a paid collaborative commission worth USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, published through Atmos and Textile Exchange and shown at the 2026 conference. Two runners-up: work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, plus a USD 2,000 cash prize.

Duration: One-off commission/prize.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Fashion Regenerated is an open call from Textile Exchange and Atmos. Winners are selected by a panel of expert judges and, for the first time, a public vote. The shortlist is promoted through Textile Exchange and Atmos, amplifying inspirational stories to an engaged audience of thousands. The overall winner receives a paid collaborative commission to the value of USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, working closely with the team to identify the right story and access; the winning work is published through Atmos, Textile Exchange, and shown to an audience of more than 1,300 changemakers at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference. Two runners-up have their work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, and there is also a USD 2,000 cash prize. Check the call site for full eligibility and submission details. Deadline: 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CASA PIP Residency for Filmmakers
item.description:
Organisation: CASA PIP

Location: Portugal; open internationally to filmmakers.

Award: Free residency (no cost to attend), offering time, space, and peace to work on a project or an early idea. A EUR 35 application fee applies.

Duration: Residency

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

CASA PIP is a residency program in Portugal founded and run by filmmakers. It encourages residents to slow down and play, providing filmmakers with the time, peace, space, and general "rulelessness" to move meaningfully forward on their work, whether it is a whole project or just the budding idea for one. The residency is free to attend. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to filmmakers. There is a EUR 35 application fee. Deadline: July 31, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kometa Fellows 2026: Field Journalism, Photography, and Writing
item.description:
Organisation: Kometa (French literary and journalism magazine)

Location: Remote / field-based (international)

Award: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. No application fee.

Duration: One year of funding for field work and research

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Kometa, a French literary and journalism magazine, awards fellowships to journalists, writers, and photographers documenting reality in contexts of war, censorship, or severe social and political tensions. Projects must be rooted in direct field experience and focused on contemporary rather than historical events. Projects already under a contractual agreement are ineligible. AMOUNT: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. WHAT FELLOWS RECEIVE: one year of funding; publication of the completed project (partial or full) in Kometa; opportunity to contribute to the monthly newsletter; mentorship through editorial collaboration. 2026 FOCUS AREAS: disinformation and narrative wars; political and social changes in the Gulf. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Tally form (https://tally.so/r/J9XxMK), in French or English (about 30 to 45 minutes). Contact: fellows@kometarevue.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lee Ufan Arles: Prix Art & Environnement 2026 (Residency + Exhibition Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain

Location: Arles, France (residency and exhibition)

Award: Funded residency (production space + accommodation) in Arles. No cash prize amount specified. No application fee.

Duration: 6–8 weeks residency in early 2027, followed by a solo summer 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Prix Art & Environnement (4th edition), awarded by Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain, supports an artist working at the intersection of art and the natural environment. The prize winner receives a funded 6–8 week production residency in Arles in early 2027, including production space, accommodation, and mentorship from Lee Ufan Arles staff, culminating in a solo summer 2027 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists of any discipline and nationality, with no age or degree requirements; candidates must demonstrate at least one solo exhibition and one or more group exhibitions. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Google Form linked from the Lee Ufan Arles website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AdK / EMS Berlin-Stockholm Bilateral Residency Program 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Studio für Elektroakustische Musik, Akademie der Künste (Berlin) and Elektronmusikstudion EMS (Stockholm)

Location: Berlin, Germany and Stockholm, Sweden; travel and accommodation covered for stays in both cities.

Award: Travel and accommodation costs for stays in Berlin and Stockholm covered; fees paid for presentation at KONTAKTE Festival 2027 (Berlin) and for presentation in Stockholm. Amount not publicly stated. No application fee.

Duration: Flexible, by agreement; works to be presented at KONTAKTE 2027 in Berlin and at a Stockholm presentation.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

A new bilateral residency collaboration between the Studio für Elektroakustische Musik at the Akademie der Künste in Berlin and Elektronmusikstudion EMS in Stockholm. Four artists will be selected to develop and present new works in both cities, with presentations at KONTAKTE 2027 in Berlin and at a corresponding event in Stockholm. The program supports new artistic and technical initiatives at the intersection of sound, music, and technology in the field of time-based, performative, and digital arts. ELIGIBLE CATEGORIES: (1) new instrumental and live-electronic works; (2) acousmatic music, computer music, radio art, and other forms of electroacoustic music and sound art; (3) projects from artists of any discipline for whom sound is a central element; (4) curatorial, publishing, and educational projects in sound-based art practice, particularly those relating in content to the AdK Studio or EMS; (5) independent research in acoustics, computer science, or musicology -- specifically software development, spatialization, or network technologies. Open to individuals and groups. Project duration is flexible and arranged by agreement. SUPPORT: travel and accommodation costs for Berlin and Stockholm covered; presentation fees paid for both venues. Selection is made by the director and advisory board of the Studio für Elektroakustische Musik and the director of EMS; decision is final with no legal entitlement to funding. Incomplete or late applications are not considered. This call supplements the Studio's regular residency program (separate deadline June 30, 2026 for July-December 2026 period). Apply exclusively via the online form at https://nc.adk.de/index.php/apps/forms/s/HD2nxbzqwgeMHWN6xHg9bQrA. Questions: estudio@adk.de. Deadline: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Revitalizing Rituals 2026 Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: Homo Faber Alapítvány (Homo Faber Foundation)

Location: Bhar Lazreg, Tunisia and Somogy County, Hungary; multi-stage in-person residency across both sites.

Award: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-stage residency: Placemaking Camp (1 week, November 2026, Hungary); Storytellers Camp (1 week, March 2027, Tunisia); Residency Period (minimum 1 month, April-July 2027); public presentation (August 2027); Closing Event (3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary).

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Revitalizing Rituals invites artists from all disciplines -- sound, movement, visual arts, installation, performance, and transdisciplinary practices -- to join a research-creation journey across two landscapes: Bhar Lazreg in Tunisia and the wetlands of Somogy County in Hungary. The project explores rituals as living forms of belonging: reconnecting communities, land, memory, food, ecological reciprocity, and the more-than-human world. Selected artists work with local communities as co-participants in a shared inquiry into moving from extraction toward reciprocity. Organised by Homo Faber Alapítvány (Hungary). AMOUNT: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. ELIGIBILITY: minimum 5 years of active artistic practice; portfolio reflecting interest in situated or relational work; no formal degree required; working language is English (local language navigation valued); must be available for the full residency cycle including all international travel; must be able to legally receive the fee and invoice the organising partners from country of residence. Strongly encourages applications from historically marginalised or underrepresented voices. KEY DATES: Application Deadline 19 July 2026 (midnight) -- note the open call PDF also references a 5 July date; the officially promoted deadline is 19 July; Online Q&A 29 June 2026, 14:00 Tunisia / 15:00 CET (register at https://forms.gle/brnZvXvFe4JB8zL26); Online Conversations with shortlisted artists: mid July to mid August 2026; Selection Results: 31 August 2026; Placemaking Camp: 1 week, end of November 2026, Hungary; Storytellers Camp: 1 week, March 2027, Tunisia; Residency Period: minimum 1 month (splittable), April to July 2027; Final Presentation: August 2027; Closing Event: 3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary. Apply via the open call form linked at https://homofaber.hu/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Artist-Open-Call-FINAL-in-the-RR-form.pdf.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jamverse Jam (Protocolized worldbuilding contest)
item.description:
Organisation: Protocolized (Summer of Protocols)

Location: Online / Worldwide

Award: One USD 1,000 grand prize plus at least 10 additional USD 200 prizes; winning artifacts are published on the Jamverse website. No entry fee.

Duration: One-off contest; submit up to three artifacts by 31 July 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Protocolized's fourth contest invites artists and writers (storytellers, poets, comic artists) to build, extend, and connect the Jamverse, a shared near-future science fiction universe of four intersecting story cycles (The Stockton Chronicles, Legends and Ledgers, UET-Trainverse, and Zoothesia). ELIGIBILITY: open to anyone; you may submit up to three in-universe worldbuilding artifacts, and the same creator can win multiple prizes. REQUIREMENTS: each artifact must be under 1,000 words (200-700 is the sweet spot) or a visual equivalent (comic panel, poster, advertisement, etc.) and must include at least one element drawn from one of the four sub-world 'toy boxes', a surprising 'traffic jam' (an emergent, ah-ha consequence of a technology or protocol), and a relation to the theme of Stigmergy (indirect coordination through modifying a shared environment). Artifact types can include comic panels, job postings, poems, forum threads, historic documents, or encyclopedia entries. APPLY: submit each artifact as a public Google Doc via the contest form, including the artifact, a one-paragraph explanation of the worlds/toy-box components used and how the piece relates to stigmergy and explores a traffic jam, and, if AI was used, documentation of your AI/LLM protocol. PRIZES: one USD 1,000 grand prize and at least 10 USD 200 prizes, all published on the Jamverse website. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. Contest rules, the current Jamverse stories, and starter materials are on the Jamverse website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World (Joint Funding Call)
item.description:
Organisation: Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, the Advanced Research & Invention Agency (ARIA), the Cooperative AI Foundation, and Google.org

Location: Global; open worldwide, with collaborations across geographic boundaries encouraged.

Award: Two funding tiers (applicants may apply to either or both), projects 1 to 2 years. Tier 1: up to USD 300,000 for exploratory research, pilot studies, or focused technical investigations. Tier 2: USD 300,000 to USD 1,000,000 for more ambitious or collaborative projects. For projects funded by Schmidt Sciences, indirect costs must be at or below 10%. No application fee.

Duration: 1 to 2 years

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

A joint philanthropic funding call by Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, ARIA, the Cooperative AI Foundation and Google.org to catalyse the foundational scientific research needed to understand, evaluate and control risks emerging from large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents deployed by multiple actors (multi-principal, multi-agent settings). The call argues that focusing only on the safety and alignment of individual models is insufficient, and seeks system-level approaches. RESEARCH AGENDA (four clusters): (1) Sandboxes and Testbeds, realistic, reproducible multi-agent environments for studying populations of frontier-model agents; (2) The Science of Agent Networks, how collective capabilities emerge and scale, how networks fail, and how dangerous population-level properties can be detected; (3) Strengthening Agent Infrastructure, evaluating and stress-testing primitives such as identity, verifiability, reputation, communication and commitment; (4) Multi-Agent Oversight and Control, detection, attribution, security and intervention methods to keep deployed agent populations safe at scale. Proposals may target one cluster or span several, with priority on depth over breadth and on realistic sandboxes and testbeds. OUT OF SCOPE: single-agent safety, capability advancement without a clear safety motivation, toy systems, naive application of pre-existing solutions, commercial product development, and purely conceptual/policy work. ELIGIBILITY: individual researchers, research teams, research institutions and multi-institution collaborations across universities, national laboratories, institutes and nonprofit research organisations; open globally. Informational webinars on 30 June and 23 July 2026; notification of decisions in autumn 2026. Proposals due 8 August 2026 by 11:59pm Anywhere on Earth. Contact: multiagentsafety@schmidtsciences.org. Apply at https://schmidtsciences.smapply.io/prog/scaling_ai_safety_for_a_multi_agent_world/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Spencer Foundation: Vision Grants 2026 (Research Planning Grants)
item.description:
Organisation: Spencer Foundation

Location: Worldwide; PI must be affiliated with a non-profit or public/governmental institution (US 501(c)(3) or non-US equivalent) that will serve as the administering organization. Proposals accepted from the US and internationally; English only; budgets in US Dollars.

Award: USD 75,000 total per grant; no indirect cost charges permitted. No application fee.

Duration: 12 to 18 months (research planning grant)

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Vision Grants are research planning grants that give scholars and their collaborators the time, space, resources and support to plan a large-scale study or program of research focused on transforming education systems toward greater equity. Rather than a fully fleshed-out research plan, the proposal is an invitation to think forward about what research is needed to transform education systems toward equity and how that systems change will happen, identifying the system(s) targeted and the specific levers the team thinks must be engaged. Grants bring together a team for 12 to 18 months to collaboratively develop ambitious, large-scale, cross-disciplinary research projects co-designed with practitioners, policymakers, communities and other partners. Awarded teams also join a cohort learning program held in person in Chicago. ELIGIBILITY: PIs and Co-PIs must have appropriate experience or an earned doctorate in an academic discipline or a terminal degree in a professional field; graduate students may be on the team but cannot be PI or Co-PI. The PI must be affiliated with a non-profit or public/governmental institution willing to serve as the administering organization (colleges, universities, school districts, research facilities, or other 501(c)(3) non-profits, or non-US equivalents); the Spencer Foundation does not award grants directly to individuals. Open to applicants in the US and internationally; proposals in English, budgets in USD. PIs and Co-PIs may apply even with another active Spencer grant or proposal in review, but may not be part of more than one Vision Grant proposal. Note: a Vision Grant is a prerequisite for applying to Spencer's Transformative Research Grant program (TRG, USD 3.5 million), though receiving one does not guarantee a TRG. PROCESS: applications opened 4 June 2026. A required Intent to Apply form (max 200 words, non-binding) is due 12 August 2026 at 12:00 PM noon Central time; the Full Proposal (2000-word narrative) is due 16 September 2026 at 12:00 PM noon Central time. Program contact: Jasmine Knetl, visiongrants@spencer.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Doha Film Festival 2026: Call for Submissions (Short Films)
item.description:
Organisation: Doha Film Institute (DFI)

Location: Doha, Qatar (festival 19-27 November 2026; at least one film representative must attend in person)

Award: Cash awards (shared between producer and director for Best Film prizes). International Short: Best Film USD 20,000, Best Director USD 12,000, Best Performance USD 7,000. Ajyal: Best Feature Film USD 35,000, Best Short Film USD 12,000. Made in Qatar: Best Film USD 15,000, Best Director USD 10,000, Best Performance USD 5,000. International Feature (invitation only): Best Narrative USD 75,000, Best Documentary USD 50,000, Best Artistic Achievement USD 45,000, Best Performance USD 15,000. No entry fee.

Duration: Festival takes place 19-27 November 2026

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

The Doha Film Festival (Doha Film Institute), running 19-27 November 2026, is accepting submissions, which are open for SHORT FILMS ONLY (feature films are selected by invitation only). Submittable competitions: the International Short Film Competition (new, daring short-form work, all genres, maximum 20 minutes, MENA premiere required) and the Ajyal Film Competition short-film category (youth-focused films, maximum 20 minutes, Middle East premiere excluding North Africa, curated with a jury of young people aged 16-25). The Made in Qatar section is open to narrative and documentary short films connected to Qatar (Qatari national or resident writer/director/producer, majority shot in Qatar, or storyline centred on Qatar), requiring Qatar premiere status. The International Feature Film Competition (documentary and narrative, 60+ minutes, MENA premiere) is by invitation only and does not accept submissions. All non-English-language works must have English subtitles and a time-coded English dialogue list; as Qatar is bilingual, films must be accessible in Arabic and English (the Festival can provide Arabic subtitles). KEY DATES: short-film submissions open 10 May 2026; deadline for International Short and Ajyal Short Competitions is 1 August 2026; Made in Qatar deadline is 1 September 2026; invited filmmakers notified by 1 October 2026. No entry fee. Submit via the entry form with a private (password-protected) link to the film. Enquiries: entries@dohafilm.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AXS Film Fund Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: AXS Film Fund

Location: Remote (worldwide; English-language application; non-English projects must have English subtitles)

Award: Up to $10,000 per grant; up to 5 creators awarded (aim to support 3-5 artists in this round). Application Assistance Stipends also available on a first-come, first-served basis to hire an application assistant. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (any stage of production: pre-production, production, post-production, or distribution)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Grants from the AXS Film Fund supporting documentary and nonfiction new media creators, particularly those who identify as living with a disability and especially from underserved communities, though applications are welcome from all creators regardless of background. Uses the Nonfiction Core Application led by the IDA and Sundance Institute. PROJECT ELIGIBILITY: feature-length documentary films (45 minutes or longer; experimental nonfiction accepted) or nonfiction new media projects with a film/video component; projects in any stage of production; non-English projects must have English subtitles and the application must be completed in English. INELIGIBLE: fiction projects (including narrative fiction based on a true story); projects where applicants are hired, employed, or commissioned by another entity; incomplete applications or those missing supporting materials; late submissions. APPLICANT ELIGIBILITY: 18 or older; not currently enrolled in a degree-granting program; individual applicants (fiscal sponsors permitted); applicant must be the director or producer of the project. SELECTION CRITERIA: strength of proposal and artistic approach; feasibility; ethics and accountability; completeness; and meeting eligibility requirements. ACCESSIBILITY: alternative application methods available (email filmfund@axslab.org by 24 July 2026); an Application Assistance Stipend is offered for 2026 to allow applicants to hire an application assistant, available first-come, first-served (not guaranteed). TIMELINE: open call opens 1 June 2026 (12:00 AM EST); deadline 31 July 2026 (11:59 PM EST). Apply via SurveyMonkey Apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MUUS Collection: Research Fellowship 2026-2027 (Remote, USA)
item.description:
Organisation: MUUS Collection

Location: Remote / hybrid; archive visits to Tenafly, New Jersey, USA

Award: USD 20,000 grant plus full coverage of travel costs to the archive. No application fee.

Duration: One year, November 2026 - November 2027

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

MUUS Collection (an American 20th-century photography collection that preserves, researches and reveals work from the archives it owns and represents) launches its first Research Fellowship, inviting a curator or academic to spend a year with the archives to develop an exhibition or publication concept offering a new perspective. The Research Fellow examines physical works and ephemera (journals, contact sheets, cameras, the totality of each photographer's collection) and benefits from the new MUUSEUM online research portal. PROJECT PERIOD: November 2026 to November 2027 (project completes November 2027). ELIGIBILITY: minimum five years professional experience at museums, galleries, universities or similar cultural institutions; international candidates eligible but fluency in English (written and spoken) is required; candidates must be willing to travel to Tenafly, New Jersey for up to a week (mutually agreed dates) and be available for remote collaboration with the archive team and advisory board. Candidates from underrepresented or marginalised communities are encouraged to apply. AWARD: USD 20,000 grant plus full coverage of travel costs to the archive. ANNOUNCEMENT: winner decided in October 2026 and announced at Paris Photo. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Beautiful Bizarre Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Beautiful Bizarre Magazine

Location: Worldwide (exhibition in San Francisco, USA)

Award: Grand Prize $10,000 plus category awards (e.g. Imaginative Realism $5,000) and a dedicated Digital Art Award and Emerging Artist Award; $76,000+ total in cash and prizes. NOTE: $40 entry fee per submission.

Duration: Annual prize (exhibition follows)

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

International, non-acquisitive art prize across seven categories including a dedicated Digital Art Award, with cash prizes and a San Francisco gallery exhibition. Grand Prize is $10,000, with category awards and over $76,000 in total cash and prizes. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally with no geographic restriction; all static media; includes an Emerging Artist Award. NOTE: there is a $40 entry fee per submission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Day Job: Short Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Day Job Films

Location: Worldwide applicants; all production must take place in the UK

Award: Production budget of up to GBP 7,000, with full creative control and no producer or external interference. No application fee.

Duration: Per short film project

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

Short film competition from Day Job Films created to give auteur directors full creative freedom, awarding a production budget of up to GBP 7,000 to make a short film with no producer interference or commercial agenda. Open to animation, live-action and documentary projects. Runs in three rounds: (1) a pitch deck outlining concept, story, key characters and visual style; (2) a full script for those who progress; and (3) an interview to discuss the vision and execution. A panel of industry professionals judges the pitch deck, script and interview, with at least one team member reading each pitch deck and script in full. The winner receives full creative control over the project. ELIGIBILITY: applicants can be from anywhere worldwide, but all production must take place in the UK; applicants must have a producer on board who is separate from the director/writer. Applicants may optionally include a plan for adapting the script into a feature-length film (this does not affect the outcome). Submissions open February 10, 2026 and close August 10, 2026. Enquiries: hello@dayjobfilms.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
